target.name: Silicon Box
target.founder: Han Byung Joon, Sehat Sutardja, Weili Dai

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $200M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $529M - $11.0B.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B

private.name: Positron AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Silicon Box is a Series B semiconductor company founded by Han Byung Joon, Sehat Sutardja, and Weili Dai, having raised $200M+ to date. The valuation analysis employs a universe of 11 comparable companies—3 public (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private peers including Etched, SambaNova Systems, Positron AI, Cerebras, and PsiQuantum, all operating in the chips and semiconductors sector at similar or adjacent funding stages.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression modeling uses the $200M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yielding $380M. Peer-median analysis across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables (SambaNova Systems at $11.0B, Etched at $5.0B, d-Matrix at $2.0B, Positron AI at $1.0B, and DEEPX at $529M) produces a median valuation of $2.0B, with a range spanning $529M to $11.0B.

The combined analysis supports a valuation range of $323M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.2B. The wide dispersion—driven primarily by heterogeneity in peer stage and market positioning—reflects material uncertainty around Silicon Box's relative competitive positioning and capital intensity trajectory within an uneven frontier-tech funding environment. Stage-progression suggests conservative downside, while peer medians capture upside scenarios consistent with better-capitalized competitors.
